chunks.text: Barbara Simons: Making Votes Count - EECS at Berkeley 2005 CS Distinguished Alumna Barbara Simons (Ph.D. ’81) is the subject of a Berkeley Engineering profile celebrating the 150th anniversary of U.C. Berkeley. Simons, who is a past president of the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM), has been drawing attention to the pitfalls of electronic voting since 2003. She’s a vocal critic of electronic ballots and is board chair of Verified Voting, a non-partisan organization that advocates for reliable and secure voting practices, as well as the author of a book titled “Broken Ballots: Will Your Vote Count?” She is also a long-time champion for programs to increase diversity in computer science and engineering. Categories Alumni Diversity publications Research View Open Faculty Positions
